title: aconitum vulparia var. penninum
intro: Aconitum vulparia var. penninum, also known as wolf's bane, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, wolf's bane has been used for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which are derived from its alkaloid compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and pain, as well as treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and pneumonia, although it should be used with caution due to its toxicity. Herbal preparations of wolf's bane are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used to treat a range of ailments, but should only be administered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
